royal-caribbean.name: Royal Caribbean

soda.name: Classic Soda Package
soda.includes: Unlimited fountain soda & Coca-Cola Freestyle, souvenir cup
includesList: Unlimited fountain soda & Coca-Cola Freestyle access (retained)
Coca-Cola souvenir cup (retained for Classic Soda only after March 15, 2026)

refreshment.name: Royal Refreshment Package
refreshment.includes: Soda + specialty coffee + tea + juice + mocktails + energy drinks + water — no alcohol
includesList: Fountain soda and canned soda at bars (Freestyle access removed March 15, 2026)

deluxe.name: Deluxe Beverage Package
deluxe.priceNote: $55–$125/day — dynamic by ship & sailing (+18% gratuity). Icon/Oasis class run highest; older ships (Grandeur) run lowest. Fleet median ~$77/day pre-cruise.
deluxe.includes: Everything in Refreshment + cocktails, wine, beer, spirits (up to $14/drink)
includesList: All Refreshment items (minus Freestyle machine after March 15, 2026)
Beer, wine, cocktails & spirits up to $14 each
Pay difference for drinks above cap

coffeeCard.name: Coffee Card
coffeeCard.note: Not valid at licensed Starbucks locations. Unused punches expire at cruise end.

rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: All adults (21+) in the same stateroom must buy the same adult (alcoholic) beverage package. Non-alcoholic packages (Classic Soda, Royal Refreshment) can be purchased individually by any guest.

loyalty.voucherNote: Each voucher = one drink up to $14. Applied to most expensive drinks first. Loaded automatically onto SeaPass card. Excludes Starbucks, Bionic Bar, Desserted.
loyalty.tierThresholdNote: Tier thresholds are Crown & Anchor POINTS (1 point per cruise night for standard cabins; bonus points for suites, solo, and promotional sailings). The nightsMin/nightsMax fields approximate this for typical non-suite cruising.

policies.title: Royal Caribbean Policy (No Exceptions Since August 1, 2025)
policies.text: ALL adults (21+) in the same stateroom MUST purchase the same adult (alcoholic) beverage package. There are absolutely NO EXCEPTIONS — not for pregnancy, medical conditions, religious reasons, recovery, or designated drivers. This is strictly enforced at booking and cannot be changed onboard. Non-alcoholic packages (Classic Soda, Royal Refreshment) are exempt — can be bought individually.

policies.title: Minor Package Requirement
policies.text: When adults purchase the Deluxe Beverage Package, all minors (under 21) in the same stateroom must purchase the Royal Refreshment Package. Minors cannot purchase Deluxe.

policies.title: Coca-Cola Freestyle Access Removed from Deluxe & Refreshment (Effective March 15, 2026)
policies.text: Starting March 15, 2026, the Coca-Cola souvenir cup and access to Coca-Cola Freestyle machines is no longer included with the Deluxe Beverage Package or Royal Refreshment Package. Soda is still covered, but via canned/fountain service at bars only. A souvenir cup add-on is available onboard for $4.99. Classic Soda Package keeps Freestyle access. Bookings made on or before March 15, 2026 retain the previous benefits.

policies.title: Pre-Cruise Pricing
policies.text: Always buy drink packages pre-cruise for the best price. Pre-cruise pricing is typically 10–40% cheaper than onboard — biggest discounts when booked 90+ days before sailing. Watch for sales during Wave Season (January–March) and Black Friday.

faq.question: How do I calculate if the Royal Caribbean drink package is worth it?
faq.answer: Enter the number of drinks you expect per day by type (cocktails, beer, wine, soda, coffee). The calculator multiplies each by its average price, then compares your daily spending to the package cost. If your total exceeds the package price plus 20% gratuity, the package saves money.

faq.question: What is the break-even point for Royal Caribbean drink packages?
faq.answer: For the Deluxe Beverage Package at approximately $89/day (pre-cruise pricing), you break even at 6-7 drinks per day when factoring in the 20% automatic gratuity. The exact break-even point depends on your drink preferences — cocktails break even faster than soda.

faq.question: What's included in the Royal Caribbean Deluxe Beverage Package?
faq.answer: Cocktails, wine by the glass, beer, spirits, soda, specialty coffee, tea, fresh juice, mocktails, energy drinks, milkshakes, and bottled water. Drinks up to $14.00 per serving (before 20% gratuity) are included. Excludes bottles of wine, souvenir cups, and specialty shots over the cap.

faq.question: Do I have to buy a drink package for everyone in my stateroom?
faq.answer: Yes. Royal Caribbean requires ALL adults (21+) in the same stateroom to purchase the same adult beverage package. There are absolutely no exceptions. If adults purchase Deluxe, all minors must purchase Refreshment.

faq.question: Should I buy the drink package pre-cruise or onboard?
faq.answer: Always buy pre-cruise. Pre-cruise pricing is typically 20-30% cheaper than onboard. You can usually cancel or modify up until a few days before sailing. Watch for sales during Wave Season (January-March) and Black Friday.

faq.question: What are Crown & Anchor drink vouchers?
faq.answer: Crown & Anchor loyalty members at Diamond level (80-174 nights) get 4 free drink vouchers per day. Diamond Plus (175-699 nights) gets 5, and Pinnacle (700+ nights) gets 6. Each voucher covers one drink up to $14. If you have vouchers, the calculator adjusts your break-even point.

faq.question: What is the Refreshment Package vs. the Deluxe?
faq.answer: The Refreshment Package (~$34.50/day) covers non-alcoholic drinks: soda, specialty coffee, fresh juice, mocktails, water, and energy drinks. The Deluxe Beverage Package (~$89/day) includes everything in Refreshment plus all alcoholic beverages up to $14/drink.

faq.question: How does sea day vs. port day weighting work?
faq.answer: Sea days typically mean more onboard drinks (lounging by the pool), while port days mean fewer (you're off exploring). The calculator adjusts estimates: sea days increase by your chosen percentage (default 20%), port days decrease by the same.

royal-caribbean.quickAnswer: Most cruisers break even on the Deluxe Beverage Package at 6-7 drinks per day. Use the calculator below to find your personal break-even point.

meta.title: Royal Caribbean Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Is It Worth It?
meta.description: Compare Soda, Refreshment, Deluxe, and Coffee Card vs à-la-carte with live break-even chart. Free Royal Caribbean drink calculator with Crown & Anchor support.

_sources.note: Prices, policies, and loyalty tiers verified 2026-04-14 via full investigate pipeline (RECON → TRIAGE → DEEP → SYNTHESIS)
pricingDates: Deluxe Beverage Package fleet median ~$77/day pre-cruise (range $55–$125, dynamic by ship)
Automatic beverage gratuity: 18% (confirmed April 2026 via royalcaribbean.com FAQ)
_sources.policyDate: Stateroom beverage package uniformity enforced August 1, 2025
knownPolicyChanges: August 1, 2025: Per-stateroom uniformity rule — all adults must buy the same ALCOHOLIC package if one does. Non-alcoholic packages exempt.

March 15, 2026: Coca-Cola souvenir cup + Freestyle machine access REMOVED from Deluxe and Royal Refreshment packages. Classic Soda Package retains both. Souvenir cup add-on sold onboard for $4.99. Bookings pre-March 15 grandfathered.
_sources.gratuityNote: RCL beverage gratuity is 18% (verified 2026-04-14 via royalcaribbean.com/faq). Prior internal note claiming a raise from 18% to 20% in 2025 was INCORRECT and has been removed. The daily onboard service gratuity (housekeeping/dining, $18.50/day standard, $21/day suites as of Nov 2024) is a separate charge and does not apply to drink-package math.

carnival.name: Carnival Cruise Line

soda.name: Bottomless Bubbles
soda.includes: Unlimited Coca-Cola fountain sodas and juice for the entire cruise

refreshment.name: CHEERS! Zero Proof
refreshment.includes: Sodas, specialty coffee, tea, juice, mocktails, energy drinks, milkshakes, bottled water, NA beer, NA sparkling wine

deluxe.name: CHEERS! Beverage Program
deluxe.includes: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $20/drink + all non-alcoholic beverages. 15 alcoholic drinks/day limit.
includesList: Beer, wine, champagne by the glass
Cocktails and spirits up to $20 per drink
Pay difference for drinks above $20 cap

rules.allAdultsPolicyNote: All adults must purchase CHEERS! if one does. Bottomless Bubbles and Zero Proof do NOT require all adults to buy.

loyalty.programTransitionNote: VIFP Club members become Carnival Rewards members on June 1, 2026, with the new program officially launching September 1, 2026. Diamond-by-May-31-2026 earns lifetime Diamond status. Platinum-by-May-31-2026 gets extended through May 31, 2028.
loyalty.voucherNote: Carnival's loyalty program does not offer daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor. Platinum guests receive two free drinks at VIFP parties on 5+ night cruises and one complimentary drink at select venues — not comparable to a daily voucher.

policies.title: Carnival CHEERS! Package Policy
policies.text: If ANY adult (21+) in your stateroom purchases the CHEERS! Beverage Program, ALL adults in that stateroom must purchase it. This rule does NOT apply to Bottomless Bubbles or CHEERS! Zero Proof — those can be purchased individually.

policies.title: 15-Drink Daily Limit
policies.text: The CHEERS! package limits alcoholic drinks to 15 per person per day. Non-alcoholic drinks are unlimited. Drinks must be ordered one at a time.

policies.title: Pre-Cruise Savings
policies.text: Save $5/day per person by purchasing CHEERS! before your cruise. Pre-cruise: $69.95/day. Onboard: $74.95/day. Both plus 20% service charge.

faq.question: How do I calculate if the Carnival CHEERS! package is worth it?
faq.answer: Enter the number of drinks you expect per day by type (cocktails, beer, wine, soda, coffee). The calculator multiplies each by its average price, then compares your daily spending to the CHEERS! cost of $69.95/day + 20% service charge ($83.94 total). If your spending exceeds the package price, CHEERS! saves money.

faq.question: What is the break-even point for Carnival CHEERS!?
faq.answer: At $83.94/day (pre-cruise with service charge), you typically break even at 7-8 drinks per day depending on what you order. Cocktails at $11 break even at about 8 drinks. Beer at $7.25 needs about 12 drinks. Mix of cocktails, beer, and wine typically breaks even around 7-8 drinks.

faq.question: What's included in the Carnival CHEERS! Beverage Program?
faq.answer: Beer, wine, champagne by the glass, cocktails, and spirits priced at $20 or less per serving. Also includes unlimited non-alcoholic beverages: sodas, specialty coffee, tea, juice, energy drinks, milkshakes, bottled water, and zero-proof cocktails. Limited to 15 alcoholic drinks per day.

faq.question: Do all guests in my cabin need to buy CHEERS!?
faq.answer: For the CHEERS! (alcohol) package: yes — all adults (21+) in the same stateroom must purchase it if one person does. However, Bottomless Bubbles (soda) and CHEERS! Zero Proof can be purchased individually without requiring all adults to buy.

faq.question: What is the CHEERS! Zero Proof package?
faq.answer: A non-alcoholic alternative at $29.99/day (+ 20% service charge). Includes premium coffee, tea, sodas, juice, energy drinks, milkshakes, bottled water, non-alcoholic beer, NA sparkling wine, and handcrafted zero-proof cocktails made with Lyre's spirits. Unlike CHEERS!, it does NOT require all adults to purchase.

faq.question: What is the $20 drink cap on CHEERS!?
faq.answer: The CHEERS! package covers drinks up to $20 per serving. If you order a drink that costs more than $20 (like premium champagne or top-shelf spirits), you pay the difference between $20 and the actual price, plus the service charge on the difference. Most standard cocktails, beers, and wines fall under the $20 cap.

faq.question: Should I buy CHEERS! pre-cruise or onboard?
faq.answer: Always buy pre-cruise. You save $5/day per person compared to onboard pricing. Pre-cruise: $69.95/day ($83.94 with service charge). Onboard: $74.95/day ($89.94 with service charge). For a 7-night cruise with 2 adults, that's $70 in savings.

faq.question: Does Carnival have loyalty drink vouchers like Royal Caribbean?
faq.answer: No. Carnival's VIFP Club (transitioning to Carnival Rewards in 2026) does not offer daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor program. VIFP members may receive occasional free drinks at loyalty parties, but there are no daily vouchers that offset package value.

carnival.quickAnswer: Most cruisers break even on CHEERS! at 7-8 drinks per day (mix of cocktails, beer, and wine). The Zero Proof package breaks even at ~8 specialty drinks/day. Use the calculator below to find your personal break-even point.

meta.title: Carnival CHEERS! Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Is It Worth It?

ncl.name: Norwegian Cruise Line

soda.name: Unlimited Pop Package
soda.includes: Unlimited Coca-Cola fountain sodas for the entire cruise

refreshment.name: Unlimited Starbucks Package
refreshment.priceNote: $16.50/day (+20% gratuity) — specialty coffee only, not a full non-alcoholic package
refreshment.includes: Unlimited Starbucks drinks at onboard Starbucks plus specialty coffees at ship cafés
includesList: Unlimited Starbucks drinks at onboard Starbucks
Does NOT include soda, juice, mocktails, or water — those are à la carte
refreshment.note: NCL does not offer a non-alcoholic 'Refreshment' tier equivalent to Royal Caribbean's. Travelers who don't drink alcohol typically buy Unlimited Pop + pay à la carte for coffee, OR buy Open Bar.

deluxe.name: Unlimited Open Bar (Premium Beverage Package)
deluxe.priceNote: $99–$109/day standalone (+20% gratuity). Often bundled free via Free-at-Sea promo — see note below.
deluxe.includes: Cocktails, wine, beer, spirits, soda, juice, mocktails, specialty coffee up to $15/drink
includesList: Cocktails, wine, beer, spirits up to $15 each
Pay difference for drinks above $15 cap
No bottled water (bottled water is à la carte on Premium — Premium Plus includes it)

deluxePlus.name: Premium Plus Beverage Package
deluxePlus.priceNote: $138/day standalone (+20% gratuity). No per-drink cap. Upgrade from Premium costs ~$29/day.
deluxePlus.includes: Everything in Premium Beverage Package plus no per-drink cap, bottled wine by the glass, premium bottled water, top-shelf spirits
includesList: No per-drink price cap (vs. $15 on Premium)
Select bottles of wine (by the glass)
deluxePlus.note: Calculator models Premium Plus as metadata only for now. The math engine compares Soda / Refresh / Deluxe / à la carte; Premium Plus ranking is UI-side.

coffeeCard.name: Coffee Card
coffeeCard.note: NCL does not offer an à-la-carte punchcard coffee card. The closest equivalent is the $16.50/day Unlimited Starbucks Package.

rules.allAdultsPolicyNote: If one adult in a cabin buys Premium (Open Bar), ALL adults in that cabin — and additional staterooms under the same method of payment — must purchase it. Stricter than RCL's per-cabin rule.
rules.minorsForceSodaNote: When adults in the cabin buy the Unlimited Open Bar, all minors (under 21) must purchase the Unlimited Pop Package. Math engine currently does NOT enforce this (planned Phase 2) — the UI warns via the 'minors-forced-soda' policy below.

loyalty.voucherNote: Latitudes Rewards has NO daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor. Silver+ tier members receive a 40% discount on pre-purchase of the Unlimited Open Bar Package, applied once at booking — not per day.

freeAtSea.serviceChargeNote: Free-at-Sea charges a FLAT $28.50/person/day service charge (26.1% effective rate on the $109 retail) — NOT the 20% gratuity that applies to standalone purchases. For a 7-night cruise for 2 adults that is $399 in gratuity alone on a 'free' package.
freeAtSea.guests3to8Note: Guests 3-8 in the cabin do NOT receive Free-at-Sea drink perks. Minor guests 1-2 get the Soda Package substitution at $12.50/day; minors 3-8 pay separately.

includes: 2 nights specialty dining (varies by sail length)
Open bar service on Great Stirrup Cay (private island)
freeAtSeaPlus.note: Launched for sailings departing Feb 2, 2026 and later. Replaces the retired 'More at Sea' program.

policies.title: 'Free at Sea' Is Not Free
policies.text: Free-at-Sea bundles the Premium Beverage Package into the fare but charges a FLAT $28.50/person/day service charge — higher than the 20% you'd pay on standalone. A 7-night cruise for 2 adults costs $399 in gratuity alone. Budget accordingly.

policies.title: Great Stirrup Cay Exclusion (Effective March 1, 2026)
policies.text: Beverage packages no longer cover drinks on Great Stirrup Cay (NCL's private Bahamian island). To get open bar on the island, upgrade to Free-at-Sea Plus ($49.99/day). This is a brand-new policy — most itineraries include a GSC stop, so factor one unpowered package day into the math.

policies.title: All Adults Must Have the Same Package
policies.text: If one adult in a cabin buys Premium (Open Bar), ALL adults in that cabin — and in additional staterooms booked under the same method of payment — must purchase it. Stricter than Royal Caribbean's per-cabin rule.

policies.title: Minors Must Buy the Soda Package
policies.text: When adults buy the Open Bar package, all minors (under 21) in the cabin must purchase the Unlimited Pop Package at $12.50/day. Guests 1-2 under 21 get this as a Free-at-Sea substitution; guests 3-8 pay separately.

policies.title: Free-at-Sea Applies to First Two Guests Only
policies.text: The Free-at-Sea drink perk is applied to the first two guests on the reservation. Guests 3-8 do not automatically get the beverage package — they pay for their own drinks or packages separately.

policies.title: Premium Plus Removed the $15 Cap
policies.text: NCL's Premium Plus Beverage Package ($138/day standalone) has no per-drink price cap — covers essentially all drinks by the glass. If you typically order cocktails above $15, Premium Plus may pay for itself.

policies.title: Onboard Starbucks Is a Separate Package
policies.text: Specialty coffee at NCL-operated cafés is included in Premium. Drinks at onboard Starbucks locations are NOT. To get Starbucks included, buy the Unlimited Starbucks Package ($16.50/day) or upgrade to Premium Plus.

policies.title: Pride of America — Hawaii Beverage Package
policies.text: Pride of America (the US-flagged Hawaii ship) sells a distinct 'Hawaii Beverage Package' with the same $109/day and $15 cap structure. NCL reserves the right to restrict alcohol service in Hawaiian ports under state liquor law.

policies.title: No Daily Drink Vouchers
policies.text: Latitudes Rewards does not offer daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor. Silver tier and above receive a 40% discount on the Unlimited Open Bar Package — applied once at booking, not per day.

faq.question: Is the NCL Free-at-Sea drink package really free?
faq.answer: No. While the package itself is $0, NCL charges a flat $28.50 per person per day service charge. For a 7-night cruise with 2 adults, that's $399 in 'gratuities' on a $0 package — equivalent to ~$21/person/day. Factor this into your decision, especially compared to the standalone $109/day + 20% = $130.80/day price.

faq.question: What's the break-even point on the NCL Premium Beverage Package?
faq.answer: Standalone at $130.80/day (with gratuity), you break even at roughly 9 cocktails/day, 16 beers, or a mixed 10-drink day. Under Free-at-Sea with the $28.50/day flat charge, break-even drops to ~2 cocktails/day — much easier to justify if you drink at all.

faq.question: What's the difference between Premium and Premium Plus?
faq.answer: Premium ($109/day) caps drinks at $15/serving — pay the difference on anything above. Premium Plus ($138/day) has no per-drink cap, includes select bottled wine by the glass, premium bottled water, and top-shelf spirits. Premium Plus is worth it if your usual cocktail costs more than $15.

faq.question: Do I have to buy a drink package for everyone in my cabin?
faq.answer: Yes. If one adult in your cabin buys the Open Bar, ALL adults must buy it — and if you booked additional staterooms under the same payment method, those adults must too. Minors under 21 must buy the Unlimited Pop Package.

faq.question: Does the package work at Great Stirrup Cay?
faq.answer: Not anymore. Effective March 1, 2026, the standard Free-at-Sea beverage package no longer covers drinks on Great Stirrup Cay (NCL's private island in the Bahamas). To get open bar on the island, upgrade to Free-at-Sea Plus at $49.99/day. Most Caribbean itineraries stop at GSC for one day — factor that into your math.

faq.question: Does Latitudes Rewards give me free drinks?
faq.answer: No daily vouchers like Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor. Silver tier (30+ nights) and above get 40% off pre-purchase of the Unlimited Open Bar Package — a one-time discount applied at booking, not per day.

faq.question: What about the Unlimited Starbucks Package?
faq.answer: $16.50/day covers unlimited Starbucks drinks at onboard Starbucks locations plus specialty coffees at ship cafés. It's the closest NCL equivalent to a non-alcoholic 'refreshment tier' but narrower — it does NOT include soda, juice, mocktails, or water. Most travelers who don't drink alcohol buy the Unlimited Pop Package ($12.50/day) and pay for coffee à la carte.

faq.question: Should I buy the drink package pre-cruise or onboard?
faq.answer: Pre-cruise, always — you save about 10% and the NCL website often runs additional promotions. Package prices can go up after embarkation. If you have Free-at-Sea bundled, you don't need to decide — the service charge is the same whether you pre-book or not.

ncl.quickAnswer: On Free-at-Sea, break-even on the Open Bar is ~2 cocktails/day because you're only paying the $28.50/day gratuity. Standalone ($109/day + 20%), break-even jumps to ~9 drinks. Premium Plus at $138/day is worth it only if your usual cocktail costs more than $15. Use the calculator below to find your personal break-even.

meta.title: NCL Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Free-at-Sea Worth It?
meta.description: Compare Premium, Premium Plus, Starbucks, and Pop Package with real 2026 Free-at-Sea service charges. Free NCL drink calculator that shows what 'free' actually costs.

celebrity.name: Celebrity Cruises

soda.name: Included in Fare
soda.priceNote: Basic soda, water, drip coffee, juice, and lemonade are included in Celebrity's base fare. No separate soda package is sold.
soda.note: Celebrity includes basic non-alcoholic drinks in the cruise fare. This is modeled as a $0 'soda package' so the calculator correctly shows these drinks at no cost.

refreshment.name: Zero Proof Package
refreshment.priceNote: $30–$45/day (+20% gratuity). Prices vary by ship and sailing.
refreshment.includes: Premium non-alcoholic drinks: specialty coffee, premium teas, fresh juices, mocktails, smoothies, premium bottled water, energy drinks

deluxe.name: Classic Beverage Package
deluxe.includes: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $10/drink + all Zero Proof items
includesList: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $10 per drink
Pay difference for drinks above $10 cap
Most domestic beers covered; many craft/imported exceed cap

deluxePlus.name: Premium Beverage Package
deluxePlus.includes: Everything in Classic + premium spirits, wines, cocktails up to $17/drink
includesList: Premium spirits up to $17 per drink
Premium wines by the glass up to $17
Craft cocktails and specialty martinis up to $17
Pay difference for drinks above $17 cap
deluxePlus.note: Calculator models Premium as metadata only. Math engine compares soda/refresh/deluxe (Classic); Premium ranking is planned Phase 2.

coffeeCard.name: Coffee Card
coffeeCard.note: Celebrity does not offer an à-la-carte coffee card. Specialty coffee is included in the Zero Proof, Classic, and Premium packages. Elite Plus Captain's Club members get free specialty coffees.

rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: All adults sharing a stateroom must purchase the same beverage package. Medical exemptions require a phone call to Celebrity. Different package tiers (Classic vs Premium) also require phone booking.
rules.minorsForceSodaNote: Minors under 21 cannot purchase alcohol packages. They receive the fare-included basic drinks. No forced package purchase required.

loyalty.voucherNote: Captain's Club does not offer daily drink vouchers. Elite members (300+ pts) get free drinks 5–7pm daily from the Captain's Club menu at most bars. Elite Plus (750+ pts) get free specialty coffees all day. Not modeled as vouchers in the calculator.

perks.drinkPerks: Free drinks 5–7pm daily at CC bars
Free specialty coffees all day + free drinks 5–7pm

drinks.name: Fresh Juice
drinks.label: Fresh Juice / Smoothie
drinks.note: Basic juice included in fare; premium smoothies covered by Zero Proof

policies.title: All Adults Must Buy the Same Package
policies.text: Every adult sharing a stateroom must purchase the same beverage package. Medical exemptions require calling Celebrity directly. Booking different tiers (Classic vs Premium) also requires a phone call.

policies.title: Classic Package $10 Cap Is Very Low
policies.text: The Classic package only covers drinks up to $10. Most craft beers ($13–$15), cocktails ($12–$17), and wines ($9–$40) exceed this cap. You'll pay the difference on almost every cocktail. Consider Premium ($17 cap) if you plan to order cocktails.

policies.title: Basic Drinks Included in Your Fare
policies.text: Celebrity includes fountain soda, drip coffee, tea, juice, lemonade, and infused water in the base fare. You don't need any package for these basics.

faq.question: What's the difference between Classic and Premium drink packages on Celebrity?
faq.answer: Classic ($89/day) covers drinks up to $10 per serving — enough for domestic beer and basic wine, but most cocktails exceed this. Premium ($109/day) covers up to $17 — enough for most standard cocktails and craft beers. Both add 20% gratuity. If you drink cocktails, Premium is almost always the better value.

faq.question: Are basic drinks free on Celebrity?
faq.answer: Yes. Fountain soda, drip coffee, tea, juice, lemonade, and infused water are included in every Celebrity fare — no package needed. The packages cover premium items: specialty coffee, cocktails, wine, beer, and spirits.

faq.question: Does Celebrity have drink vouchers like Royal Caribbean?
faq.answer: No daily vouchers. Captain's Club Elite members (300+ pts) get free drinks 5–7pm daily. Elite Plus (750+ pts) also get free specialty coffees all day. These aren't comparable to RCL's per-day voucher system.

faq.question: Why is the Classic package cap so low?
faq.answer: The $10 Classic cap was designed for beer and house wine drinkers. It predates the recent drink price inflation. Most cocktails ($12–$17) and craft beers ($13–$15) exceed $10, making Classic a poor value for cocktail drinkers. If you plan to order mixed drinks, the Premium package ($17 cap) or à la carte is usually better.

celebrity.quickAnswer: Celebrity includes basic soda, coffee, and juice in the fare — no package needed for those. The Classic package ($89/day) has a low $10 cap that most cocktails exceed. Premium ($109/day) at $17 cap is the sweet spot for cocktail drinkers. Use the calculator to see which works for your mix.

meta.title: Celebrity Cruises Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Classic vs Premium
meta.description: Compare Classic ($10 cap), Premium ($17 cap), Zero Proof, and à-la-carte. Celebrity includes basic drinks free — find out if upgrading is worth it.

pricingDates: Gratuity: 20% on all packages and individual drinks
_sources.gratuityNote: 20% automatic service charge on all beverage packages and à-la-carte drink purchases.

princess.name: Princess Cruises

soda.name: Included in Fare
soda.priceNote: Princess Standard fare includes juice (breakfast), milk, iced water, iced tea, hot tea, drip coffee, and lemonade. No separate soda package sold.

refreshment.name: Princess Plus (non-alcoholic portion)
refreshment.priceNote: Princess does not sell a non-alcoholic-only package. The cheapest package covering premium drinks is Princess Plus ($65/day). Modeled at Plus price so the calculator correctly shows Plus as the entry point.
refreshment.includes: Specialty coffee, premium tea, mocktails, Red Bull, bottled water, fountain soda — all included in Princess Plus

deluxe.name: Princess Plus
deluxe.priceNote: $60–$70/day (+18% gratuity when purchased separately; included in fare when booked as Plus fare). Sphere-class ships (Sun/Star Princess) add ~$5/day.
deluxe.includes: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $15/drink + specialty coffee, tea, mocktails, Red Bull, bottled water, 25% off wine bottles. 15 alcoholic drinks/day limit.
includesList: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $15 each

deluxePlus.name: Princess Premier
deluxePlus.priceNote: $85–$105/day (+18% gratuity separately; included in fare as Premier). Sphere-class +$5/day.
deluxePlus.includes: Premium spirits, reserve wines, cocktails up to $20/drink + all Plus items. 15 alcoholic/day, unlimited NA.
includesList: Reserve wines by the glass up to $20
Woodford Reserve and Jack Daniel's selections
deluxePlus.note: Calculator models Premier as metadata only. Math engine compares soda/refresh/deluxe (Plus); Premier ranking is planned Phase 2.

coffeeCard.name: Coffee Card
coffeeCard.note: Princess does not offer a coffee card. Specialty coffee is included in Princess Plus and Premier packages.

rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: Unlike most cruise lines, Princess allows individual adults to purchase drink packages independently. One person can buy Plus while their cabinmate buys nothing.

loyalty.voucherNote: Captain's Circle does not offer daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor.

drinks.name: Fresh Juice
drinks.label: Fresh Juice
drinks.note: Juice at breakfast included in fare

policies.title: Individual Package Purchase Allowed
policies.text: Unlike Royal Caribbean and Carnival, Princess allows individual adults to buy drink packages independently. One person in the cabin can have Plus while the other pays à la carte.

policies.title: 15 Alcoholic Drinks Per Day Limit
policies.text: Both Plus and Premier limit alcoholic beverages to 15 per day (6am–6am). Non-alcoholic drinks also count toward the Plus limit but NOT the Premier limit. Specialty coffees/teas don't count for either.

policies.title: Sphere-Class Ships Cost ~$5 More
policies.text: Sun Princess and Star Princess (Sphere class) charge approximately $5/day more for Plus and Premier packages compared to the rest of the fleet.

faq.question: What's the difference between Princess Plus and Premier?
faq.answer: Plus ($65/day) covers drinks up to $15 each with a 15 alcoholic drink limit and specialty coffees included. Premier ($100/day) covers up to $20 per drink, adds top-shelf spirits and reserve wines, and makes non-alcoholic drinks unlimited (no daily limit). Both include 25% off wine bottles.

faq.question: Do both people in the cabin need to buy the same package?
faq.answer: No! Princess is one of the few cruise lines that lets you buy a drink package individually. One adult can have Plus while the other pays à la carte.

faq.question: What's included in the Princess Standard fare?
faq.answer: Juice at breakfast, milk, iced water, iced tea, hot tea, drip coffee, and lemonade. No soda, no specialty coffee, no alcohol.

princess.quickAnswer: Princess Plus at $65/day ($15 cap, includes specialty coffee) is excellent value — better per-dollar than most competitors. Plus can be purchased individually (no forced cabin-mate rule). Premier at $100/day raises the cap to $20 for top-shelf.

meta.title: Princess Cruises Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Plus vs Premier
meta.description: Compare Princess Plus ($15 cap), Premier ($20 cap), and à-la-carte. Individual purchase allowed — no cabin-mate rule.

_sources.gratuityNote: 18% on packages purchased separately. Gratuities included when booked as Plus/Premier fare.

holland.name: Holland America Line

soda.name: Quench Package
soda.priceNote: $17.95/day pre-cruise (+18% gratuity). Non-alcoholic premium: soda, coffee, juice, mocktails, Red Bull.

refreshment.name: Signature Beverage Package
refreshment.priceNote: $50.95/day pre-cruise (+18% gratuity). All Quench items + beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $12/drink.
refreshment.includes: All Quench items + beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $12 per drink
includesList: Beer, wine, cocktails, spirits up to $12 each
Pay difference for drinks above $12 cap

deluxe.name: Elite Beverage Package
deluxe.priceNote: $65.95/day pre-cruise (+18% gratuity). All Signature items + premium spirits, wines, cocktails up to $16/drink. Unlimited zero-proof.
deluxe.includes: All Signature items + premium spirits, wines, cocktails up to $16 per drink. 15 alcoholic/day, unlimited non-alcoholic.
includesList: Premium spirits, wines, cocktails up to $16 each
Pay difference for drinks above $16 cap

coffeeCard.name: Coffee Card
coffeeCard.note: Holland America does not offer a coffee card. Specialty coffee is included in the Quench, Signature, and Elite packages.

rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: All adults in the same stateroom must purchase the same beverage package if one buys it.

loyalty.voucherNote: Mariner Society does not offer daily drink vouchers comparable to Royal Caribbean's Crown & Anchor.

policies.title: Daily Happy Hour — 50% Off Drinks
policies.text: Holland America offers happy hour most days (usually 4–5pm) at select bars (typically Ocean Bar and Billboard Onboard) where drinks are 50% off. Factor this into your break-even calculation.

policies.title: Three Package Tiers with Different Caps
policies.text: Holland America uniquely offers three alcohol-capable tiers: Quench ($0 alcohol, non-alcoholic only), Signature ($12 cap), and Elite ($16 cap). The calculator compares Quench, Signature, and Elite against à la carte.

faq.question: What's the difference between Quench, Signature, and Elite on Holland America?
faq.answer: Quench ($17.95/day) is non-alcoholic only: premium coffee, soda, juice, mocktails, Red Bull. Signature ($50.95/day) adds alcohol up to $12/drink. Elite ($65.95/day) raises the cap to $16/drink and makes zero-proof drinks unlimited. All add 18% gratuity.

faq.question: Is there a happy hour on Holland America?
faq.answer: Yes — most days 4–5pm at select bars (usually Ocean Bar and Billboard Onboard), drinks are 50% off. If you time your drinking to happy hour, you may not need a package at all.

holland.quickAnswer: Holland America's 3-tier system is unusually flexible. Quench ($17.95/day) is great value for coffee and mocktail lovers. Signature ($50.95/day) covers most standard drinks. Elite ($65.95/day) is one of the cheapest premium packages in the industry. The daily happy hour (50% off) can also shift the break-even math.

meta.title: Holland America Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Quench vs Signature vs Elite
meta.description: Compare Quench, Signature ($12 cap), and Elite ($16 cap) packages with 2026 pricing. Includes happy hour analysis.

_sources.gratuityNote: 18% service charge on all beverage purchases and packages.

msc.name: MSC Cruises

soda.name: Minors Package
soda.priceNote: $22/day (4+ nights) or $25/day (3 nights). Gratuity included. For guests under 18.
soda.includes: Non-alcoholic drinks for minors: soda, juice, water, mocktails

refreshment.name: Alcohol-Free Package
refreshment.includes: Premium non-alcoholic: specialty coffee, premium tea, mocktails, fresh juice, Red Bull, bottled water, soda

deluxe.name: Premium Extra Package
deluxe.priceNote: $85/day (4+ nights) or $95/day (3 nights). Gratuity included. All Alcohol-Free items + beer, wine, cocktails, spirits.
deluxe.includes: All Alcohol-Free items + beer, wine, cocktails, spirits. 15 alcoholic/day limit, unlimited non-alcoholic.

rules.gratuityNote: MSC includes 18% gratuity in all advertised package prices. Engine gratuity is 0% because priceMid values are already all-in.
rules.deluxeCapNote: MSC Premium Extra does not have a published per-drink cap. Most drinks on the ship fall within the package. Ultra-premium spirits may be excluded.
rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: All adults in the same cabin must purchase the same beverage package.
rules.alcoholAgeNote: MSC's drinking age is 18 on most international itineraries, 21 on North America sailings.

loyalty.voucherNote: MSC Voyagers Club does not offer daily drink vouchers.

policies.title: Gratuity Included in All Package Prices
policies.text: Unlike most cruise lines, MSC includes 18% gratuity in the advertised package price. The $85/day Premium Extra is the final price — no hidden service charge. Individual drink purchases still have 15% (Europe) or 18% (North America) added.

policies.title: Easy & Easy Plus Packages Discontinued
policies.text: MSC's former Easy ($49/day) and Easy Plus ($59/day) packages are no longer available for 2026 bookings. The current lineup is Alcohol-Free, Premium Extra, and Minors.

policies.title: Drinking Age Varies by Region
policies.text: MSC's drinking age is 18 on most international itineraries but 21 on North America sailings. This affects which guests can purchase the Premium Extra package.

faq.question: Is gratuity included in MSC drink package prices?
faq.answer: Yes — unlike Royal Caribbean, Carnival, and NCL, MSC includes 18% gratuity in the advertised package price. The $85/day Premium Extra is the final all-in daily cost. No surprise service charges.

faq.question: What happened to the MSC Easy and Easy Plus packages?
faq.answer: Discontinued for 2026. MSC simplified to three packages: Alcohol-Free ($33/day), Premium Extra ($85/day), and Minors ($22/day). If you see Easy or Easy Plus pricing online, it's outdated.

msc.quickAnswer: MSC includes gratuity in all package prices — $85/day Premium Extra is the real price, no surprises. With cocktails at $14 and wine at $13–$16, break-even is ~6 drinks/day. The $33/day Alcohol-Free is strong value for coffee and mocktail drinkers.

meta.title: MSC Cruises Drink Package Calculator 2026 | Premium Extra Worth It?
meta.description: Compare Premium Extra ($85/day all-in), Alcohol-Free, and à-la-carte. MSC includes gratuity — no hidden charges.

_sources.gratuityNote: 18% gratuity included in all MSC package prices. Individual drinks: 15% (Europe) or 18% (North America).

virgin.name: Virgin Voyages

soda.name: Included in Fare
soda.priceNote: Water, juice, soda, teas, and drip coffee are included in every Virgin Voyages fare. No package needed.

refreshment.name: No NA Package Available
refreshment.priceNote: Virgin Voyages does not sell a non-alcoholic premium package. Basic drinks are included; specialty drinks are à la carte.
refreshment.note: Sentinel price $999 ensures this option never wins in the calculator. Virgin has no NA-only package.

deluxe.name: No Drink Package Available
deluxe.priceNote: Virgin Voyages does NOT sell drink packages. All alcoholic and specialty drinks are à la carte. See the Bar Tab system below.
deluxe.note: Sentinel price $999. The calculator will always recommend à la carte for Virgin, which is the correct answer — there is no package to compare against.

barTab.description: Pre-loaded credit on your onboard account. Not a package — no cap, no daily limit, no covered-drinks list. Just prepaid money that works like a gift card at any bar.
barTab.bonusNote: Bonus amounts vary by promotion and sailing. The values above are typical 2026 offers. Once your Bar Tab balance is spent, additional drinks are charged à la carte to your onboard account.
barTab.gratuityNote: Virgin Voyages does not add automatic gratuity to drink purchases. A $13 cocktail costs $13.

rules.gratuityNote: Virgin Voyages charges NO automatic gratuity on drink purchases. Menu prices are final.
rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: No packages exist, so no cabin-mate rule applies.
rules.alcoholAgeNote: Virgin Voyages is 18+ only (no children on board).

loyalty.voucherNote: The Sailing Club does not offer drink vouchers. Repeat Sailors earn discounts on future bookings, not onboard drink credits.

policies.title: Virgin Voyages Does Not Sell Drink Packages
policies.text: There is no unlimited drink package on Virgin Voyages. All alcoholic and specialty drinks are à la carte. The calculator will always recommend à la carte — that's the only option.

policies.title: Bar Tab — Prepaid Drink Credit
policies.text: Pre-load $200–$1000 onto your onboard account and Virgin adds a bonus (typically $25–$150 depending on amount). Not a package — no cap, no daily limit. Works like a gift card at any bar. Once spent, additional drinks charge à la carte.

policies.title: No Automatic Gratuity on Drinks
policies.text: Virgin Voyages does not add 18% or 20% to your drink orders. A $13 cocktail costs $13. This makes à-la-carte math simpler than on other cruise lines.

policies.title: Adults Only (18+)
policies.text: Virgin Voyages is an adults-only cruise line. All Sailors must be 18+. No children, no minors packages.

faq.question: Does Virgin Voyages have a drink package?
faq.answer: No. Virgin is the only major cruise line without drink packages. All drinks are à la carte. Basic drinks (soda, water, juice, coffee, tea) are included in the fare. Cocktails, beer, wine, and specialty drinks are paid per drink with no hidden gratuity.

faq.question: What is the Virgin Voyages Bar Tab?
faq.answer: Pre-loaded credit ($200–$1000) on your onboard account. Virgin adds a bonus: typically $25 on $200, up to $150 on $1000. Works like a gift card — no cap, no daily limit, no covered-drinks list. Once spent, drinks charge to your cabin account.

faq.question: Are basic drinks free on Virgin Voyages?
faq.answer: Yes. Water, juice, fountain soda, teas, and drip coffee are included in every fare. Specialty coffee, fresh juice, smoothies, mocktails, and all alcohol are à la carte.

virgin.quickAnswer: Virgin Voyages has no drink packages — the calculator shows your estimated daily à-la-carte spend. Basic drinks are free. Consider pre-loading a Bar Tab ($200–$1000) for bonus credit. No gratuity on drinks makes pricing transparent.

meta.title: Virgin Voyages Drink Calculator 2026 | Bar Tab Worth It?
meta.description: No drink packages on Virgin — estimate your à-la-carte spending and see if a Bar Tab pre-load saves money.

_sources.gratuityNote: No automatic gratuity on drinks. Menu prices are final.

costa.name: Costa Cruises

soda.name: My Soft Drinks
soda.priceNote: ~€23/day. Water, coffee, juices, soft drinks, non-alcoholic cocktails.

refreshment.name: My Drinks
refreshment.priceNote: ~€35/day pre-cruise, ~€42/day onboard. Draught beer, house spirits, classic cocktails, wine with meals, soft drinks, coffee.
refreshment.includes: All Soft Drinks items + draught Heineken, house spirits, classic cocktails, wine with meals
includesList: Does NOT include premium cocktails, bottled beer, fruit juice, minibar

deluxe.name: My Drinks Plus
deluxe.priceNote: ~€48/day pre-cruise, ~€57/day onboard. Premium cocktails, prestigious spirits, bottled beer, fruit juice, minibar access.
deluxe.includes: All My Drinks items + premium cocktails, prestigious spirits, bottled beer, fruit juice, minibar

rules.gratuityNote: Costa package prices appear all-in. 15% service charge applies to individual à-la-carte drink purchases only. Engine gratuity set to 0% for package math.
rules.allAdultsSamePackageNote: All adults sharing a cabin or on the same reservation must have the same unlimited package. Children 4+ included; under-18s get soft drinks only.

loyalty.voucherNote: Costa Club does not offer daily drink vouchers.

policies.title: Prices in EUR
policies.text: Costa Cruises prices are in Euros (€), not US Dollars. Use the currency converter to see equivalent USD amounts.

policies.title: Children 4+ Included in Package
policies.text: Children aged 4 and older are included in the cabin's drink package. Under-18s receive soft drinks only. Children's packages are free when the adult package is purchased during booking.

faq.question: What's the difference between My Drinks and My Drinks Plus?
faq.answer: My Drinks (~€35/day) covers house spirits, draught beer, classic cocktails, and wine with meals. My Drinks Plus (~€48/day) adds premium cocktails, top-shelf spirits, bottled beer, fruit juice, and minibar access. If you prefer cocktails over basic beer and wine, Plus is worth the €13/day upgrade.

costa.quickAnswer: Costa's My Drinks Plus at ~€48/day is competitive for a European line. With cocktails at €5.70–€10.30, break-even is ~5–6 drinks/day. The My Soft Drinks package (€23/day) is excellent value for coffee and juice drinkers.

meta.title: Costa Cruises Drink Package Calculator 2026 | My Drinks vs Plus
meta.description: Compare My Soft Drinks, My Drinks, and My Drinks Plus with 2026 EUR pricing.

_sources.gratuityNote: Packages appear all-in. 15% service charge on individual purchases.
